payload.text:
I want you to create a one-page landing website for my brand Adhesure (a CGM overpatch brand that protects Dexcom G7 and Stelo sensors).
I have uploaded product packaging and a screenshot of a draft layout to guide the brand style — please match the colors, tone, and aesthetic.

MAIN GOALS OF THE WEBSITE

Display a popup offering 15% off the customer’s next order when they submit their email address.

Show a simple product selection section that leads to a video area with instructions, all within the same page (no additional pages).

The overall page should look clean, modern, medical-grade, and trustworthy, with soft animations for different steps.

PAGE STRUCTURE & SECTIONS

Please build the landing page with the following structure:

Header

Brand logo (Adhesure)

Simple top navigation (only anchors to sections on the same page)

Email Popup

Trigger on page load or after 5 seconds

Text: “Get 15% OFF your next order! Enter your email to receive your discount.”

Hero Section

Product packaging image

Headline: “Protect Your Dexcom G7 & Stelo Sensors With Premium Adhesive Patches”

Subheadline: “Water-resistant, long-lasting, gentle on skin”

Call-to-action button: “How to Apply”

Product Selection Section

Show the different adhesive patch variations

Simple “Select Product” or “Shop Now” buttons

Smooth scroll to the instructional section

Instruction Section (Main Content)
Add animations/icons for each step. Include the following text exactly:

How to apply your glucose adhesive patches

Follow these quick steps to get the best fit and longest wear for your Dexcom G7 and Stelo sensors.

When to Apply:

Apply after you’ve inserted a new sensor.

Or when your current sensor patch is starting to peel and you want extra hold.

Best applied right after cleaning the skin, so it sticks strong.

Skin Preparation:

Make sure the skin is clean and completely dry.

Avoid lotions or oils before applying.

Trim or shave hair around the area for better adhesion.

Do not apply over cuts, rashes, or irritated skin.

How to Apply:

Peel off the paper backing from the patch.

Place the adhesive gently over your sensor, pressing firmly around the edges.

Smooth out any wrinkles or bubbles for maximum hold.

Press for 10–15 seconds to secure.

See Exactly How to Apply it: (Insert a video box element here)

Wearing the Patches:

Patches last 7–14 days depending on activity and skin type.

Water-resistant: safe for swimming, showers, and workouts.

How to Remove the Patches:

Gently peel from one edge while holding your sensor in place.

Use an adhesive remover wipe or baby oil if needed.

Clean the area before reapplying a new patch.

Footer

Email signup (second placement)

Links: Contact, Privacy Policy, Terms

Small brand logo

DESIGN & INTERACTION REQUIREMENTS

One-page layout only (no additional pages)

Clean, medical, modern look

Light animations for each instructional step (fade-in or slide-in)

Consistent with uploaded packaging & draft design

Mobile responsive design

Include a video placeholder/box for instructions

**Designing the Adhesure Site**

I'm deep into the design phase for the Adhesure landing page. Initial analysis of the request has honed in on the key goals: a one-page design, highlighting compatibility with Dexcom G7 & Stelo, and leveraging the product packaging image. I've begun sketching out potential layouts, focusing on clear calls to action and a visually appealing presentation of the overpatch.


**Developing the UI Elements**

I've progressed to solidifying the UI elements. I'm prioritizing a clean, medical-grade aesthetic, leaning heavily on the teal/cyan color and dark backgrounds. Email popup, hero, product selection, instructions, and footer are the planned sections. I'm leaning toward React 18+, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Lucide React, and Framer Motion for the tech stack to build this out.


**Constructing the Architecture**

I'm now fully immersed in the component structure. I've mapped out the `App.tsx` container, the `Header`, `Hero`, `Popup`, `ProductSection`, `InstructionSection`, and `Footer` components. I'm also considering a reusable `Button.tsx`. I have the popup logic using `useEffect` and local state for its `isOpen` property, with the timing finalized. I have also the standard ID-based scrolling for navigation. I'm focusing on "soft animations" using `framer-motion` for a premium user experience.


**Mapping the File Structure**

I've outlined the core component file structure for the Adhesure site. I'm focusing on a component-based architecture for maintainability, including the `metadata.json` for app info, `index.html` with the Tailwind CDN, and `index.tsx` as the React 18 entry. The `App.tsx` file orchestrates the components: `Header`, `Hero`, `ProductSection`, `InstructionSection`, `Popup`, and `Footer`. Now, I'm defining the `components` directory.


**Finalizing UI Implementation**

Now I'm finalizing the UI elements.  I'm focusing on the packaging aesthetic with black backgrounds and teal text. For the instructions section, I've implemented a vertical step layout with icons. The Tailwind configuration will utilize arbitrary values for specific teal shades to match the image, sticking with standard dark mode colors otherwise.  I'm using `lucide-react` for clean medical icons, and implementing a close and submit state for the popup. The instruction text is being broken into cards for readability.


**Building the Core Structure**

I'm now fully immersed in the core structure. My latest focus is on the `Hero` section, using a background image placeholder that mirrors the packaging context—dark and abstract. I've also implemented a local storage check in the `Popup` to avoid annoying users on refresh, a simple but practical touch. Right now, I'm focusing on the colors using `bg-zinc-950` for the background and `text-teal-400` for text, mirroring the brand's aesthetic.
